paper_evaluations.title: Hydrocortisone for Preventing Adverse Drug Reactions to Snake Antivenom: A Meta-Analysis.

11.reason: Lists extracted variables: participants, ADRs, design, antivenoms, premedication regimens

12.reason: Used Cochrane ROB-2 tool for RCTs and Newcastle-Ottawa Scale for cohorts

13.reason: Principal measure stated as pooled odds ratio using random-effects model

14.reason: Describes random-effects model, subgroup analyses, sensitivity analyses methods

15.reason: Publication bias assessed using funnel plots, Begg's and Egger's tests

16.reason: Describes sensitivity analyses and multiple subgroup analyses methods

17.reason: PRISMA flow diagram shows 831 identified, 4 included with 1348 participants

20.reason: Table 1 and forest plots present individual study results with ORs

24.reason: Table 2 provides GRADE evidence quality assessment for all outcomes

25.reason: Discussion covers limitations: heterogeneity, small samples, methodological quality

26.reason: Clear conclusion: hydrocortisone alone ineffective but combinations may help

27.reason: Acknowledgments lists three funding sources with grant numbers

comparison_details.title: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis Comparing the Safety and Efficacy of Spinal Anesthesia and Spinal Anesthesia Combined with Obturator Nerve Block in Transurethral Resection of Bladder Tumors.

items.ai_reason: Title explicitly states 'A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis'
Introduction provides rationale for combining ONB with spinal anesthesia in TURBT
Objectives stated to systematically evaluate ONB + SA in TURBT to provide evidence
No mention of protocol registration (e.g., PROSPERO) or availability
Section 2.1 specifies detailed inclusion criteria for studies and participants
Lists PubMed, EMBASE, Cochrane, CNKI, Wanfang databases; January 2000-December 2021
Basic search terms provided but not full reproducible search strategy
Section 2.3 describes two independent researchers screening with third-person arbitration
Section 2.4 describes two researchers extracting data independently
Section 2.4 lists extracted data items including patient, intervention, and outcome information
Section 2.5 describes Cochrane Handbook tool and Jadad scale for bias assessment
Section 2.6 specifies MD for continuous and RR for discrete indicators
Section 2.6 describes meta-analysis methods, heterogeneity assessment, and model selection
Section 2.6 mentions funnel plot for publication bias assessment
Subgroup and sensitivity analyses described in Section 2.6
Figure 1 flow diagram shows 444 initially searched, 8 finally included
Table 1 provides detailed characteristics of all 8 included studies
Table 2 presents risk of bias assessment for each study
Forest plots show individual study results with confidence intervals
Multiple forest plots present pooled results with 95% CIs for all outcomes
Figure 7 presents funnel plot showing publication bias
Subgroup analyses by approach and sensitivity analysis results presented
Discussion summarizes main findings of reduced complications and better outcomes with SA+ONB
Three specific limitations listed at end of discussion section
Conclusion provides interpretation and calls for more high-quality multicenter studies
No funding source or financial support information reported

comparison_details.title: Efficacy of ketorolac in the treatment of acute migraine attack: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

items.ai_reason: Title explicitly includes 'systematic review and meta-analysis'
Introduction provides rationale discussing migraine burden and need to evaluate ketorolac
Objectives clearly stated: 'to evaluate the efficacy of parenteral ketorolac in treating acute migraine'
Eligibility criteria specified: RCTs comparing parenteral ketorolac to other medications
Information sources listed: CENTRAL, Medline, Google Scholar up to January 2021
Search strategy mentioned but full strategy not presented
Selection process described: two authors independently assessed eligibility
Data extraction process described using data extraction forms
Data items comprehensively listed including outcomes and participant characteristics
Risk of bias assessment described using Cochrane tool by three authors
Effect measures specified: RRs for dichotomous, SMDs for continuous outcomes
Synthesis methods described: random-effects model using RevMan 5.4
Mentions intention to use funnel plots if sufficient studies available
GRADE methodology used for certainty assessment
Study selection results provided with PRISMA flow diagram (Figure 1)
Table 1 provides comprehensive study characteristics for all included studies
Risk of bias assessment results presented in Figure 2
Individual study results presented in forest plots and text
Meta-analysis results presented with forest plots, effect estimates and CIs
Publication bias assessment mentioned but not reported
GRADE certainty assessments provided in Tables 2 and 3
Discussion section summarizes main findings and compares with previous review
Dedicated limitations section discussing sample sizes and language restrictions
Conclusions relate directly to objectives about ketorolac efficacy
Protocol registered in PROSPERO (CRD42021233911)
Funding sources mentioned for some included studies
No competing interests declaration found in the manuscript
No mention of data, code, or materials availability
